claims.claim: Spiders in the spider's lair produce silk that is considered very important.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: A priest of Slumber is located in a nearby town.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Only approximately five towns remain in the region.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: The Archmage's name is Brightbuckle (also referred to as Nalo Brightbuckle).
claims.speaker: Anzu

claims.claim: Leomund's Tiny Hut is opaque from the outside but transparent from the inside, and its exterior color can be chosen by the caster.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Elves in this setting do not sleep in the conventional sense, instead entering a trance state.
claims.speaker: Anzu

claims.claim: The walled settlement is visible from the road and is approached via a gate flanked by two guards, one on either side.
claims.speaker: Anzu

claims.claim: Anzu is described as the oldest person he has ever met, and this has been true for hundreds of years, implying he is an extraordinarily long-lived individual.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Phoenicians are a named group or faction relevant to the party's current situation.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The large map at Palomar is centered on the city of Karwin, with a fort (now labeled Palomar) to the right of Karwin, a mountain range roughly halfway up the map, Trilene added to the map, and the beginning of a body of water on the far west side.
claims.speaker: Anzu

claims.claim: Palomar was formerly known by a different name before being renamed; the old name was obscured on the map.
claims.speaker: Anzu
